channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: V Rising Developer Stunlock Is Working on a New Game, No 1.2 Update Coming
item.content:encoded:
Stunlock Studios has announced a new game set in the V Rising universe, and it'll be the developer's most ambitious project yet, apparently.

In a dev update appropriately titled "The Next Era", Stunlock describes the project as being "set in the beautiful, strange, and gothic world of V Rising", drawing on the studio's experience to explore "fresh ideas unbound by [its] previous limitations".

Stunlock says the game will feel "both familiar and strikingly new", and that it will allow you to "explore your darkest whims in a world of greater depth, danger, and mystery than ever before". The studio is, perhaps understandably, fairly reluctant to reveal more about the game at the moment, given how early on in development the project is.

Indeed, Stunlock itself says the project, whatever it turns out to be, is "very early in development", and that "things may grow quiet for a time" while the developer commits to working on the game.

Despite that, Stunlock says it will "emerge from the shadows now and then" to tell you how the project is progressing, so hopefully, we'll get to learn exactly what the studio is working on and how it relates to V Rising soon.

As for V Rising itself, Stunlock reckons it's now complete, so if you were hoping for a followup to last year's Version 1.1 update, you'll have to shelve those hopes, unfortunately.

V Rising will still receive bug fixes and balance updates, though, so if something does go catastrophically wrong with the vampire survival sim, it should still be fixed.

It seems like a good time for Stunlock to move on from V Rising; the game has managed to rack up millions of sales, and it's good to see the studio putting that money back into the development cycle.

For now, while you wait for Stunlock's next game, you can check out V Rising on PC and PlayStation 5.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
